title: Rust-specific callout in Section 2 is inconsistent appendage
description: Lines 102-103: 'Rust-specific: are lifetime annotations present that could be elided, or that signal a design that should be restructured to avoid them entirely?' This is the only language-specific note in the prompt, and it is tacked on after the section's bullets rather than integrated. The rest of the prompt uses Rust-flavored vocabulary (traits, generics, lifetimes) throughout but pretends to be language-agnostic. Fix: decide which one. Option A: commit to Rust — rename MODE to LANG-DESIGN/RUST, integrate the lifetime callout into the bullets, add more Rust-specific concerns (e.g., impl Trait vs dyn Trait tradeoffs, when to use Arc vs Rc vs owned). Option B: generalize — remove all Rust-specific vocabulary, replace with language-neutral equivalents.
close_reason: All four PROMPT-review-lang.md fixes applied in one pass.
